concept: Express / To Make the Inner Self Open
description: The act of conveying a thought or feeling in a perceivable form. It is the fundamental process of making the inner world manifest in the outer world.
sound_symbolism: The word begins with 'mi', a soft nasal that resonates from deep within, where authentic expression originates. This inner vibration flows through 'sho', a gentle fricative that transforms internal feeling into external form—the breath that carries truth outward. The final 'a' opens completely, releasing what was held inside into the shared world. This three-part movement—from inner resonance through transformative breath to complete opening—mirrors the courage of true expression. The sound progression feels like a flower unfolding: first the bud ('mi'), then the gentle unfurling ('sho'), finally the full bloom ('a').
grammatical_notes: Functions as both verb (to express) and noun (an expression) without morphological change, similar to Mandarin biǎodá (表达), Indonesian ungkapan, and Hindi abhivyakti (अभिव्यक्ति). The same form captures both the act of expressing and what is expressed.
